title: Epic EHR API Rate Limiting in Production
description: Production Epic FHIR API enforces a 60 req/min rate limit per app registration. Real-time patient matching at peak hours may exceed this threshold, causing inference latency or dropped requests.
mitigation: Implement request queuing with exponential backoff. Explore Epic bulk data API for batch nightly pre-scoring. Engage Epic technical account manager for rate limit increase request.

title: Demographic Bias in Patient Acquisition Model
description: Initial holdout validation shows 12% lower recall for Hispanic and Black patient cohorts compared to overall population. Deploying without remediation would perpetuate health equity disparities.
mitigation: Augment training set with stratified SMOTE oversampling for underrepresented cohorts. Add per-demographic fairness metrics to model card. Require CMO sign-off on bias report before pilot launch.

title: PHI Misclassification in Free-Text EHR Fields
description: De-identification pipeline was validated against structured fields only. Free-text clinical notes may contain unstructured PHI (indirect identifiers) not caught by current regex patterns.
mitigation: Deployed NLP-based PHI scanner (Presidio + custom rules) on Wk 3. Re-processed all training data through enhanced pipeline. Compliance team confirmed controls acceptable for pilot scope.

title: Staff Adoption Resistance to AI Scheduling
description: Scheduling coordinators have expressed concern about AI 'overriding' their judgment. Without adoption, the agent's recommendations will be ignored, yielding no conversion uplift.
mitigation: Embed clinical change champions in 3 pilot clinic units. Position AI as decision-support, not automation. Redesign UX to show confidence scores and override controls prominently.

title: Third-Party Referral Portal API Deprecation
description: External referral management vendor (ReferralMD) deprecated v1 API with 60-day notice. v2 migration is not in current project scope, risking broken referral ingestion at go-live.
mitigation: Evaluate direct database polling as tactical fallback. Raised as scope change with client sponsor. v2 adapter estimated at 3 engineer-days — under review for Wk 5 inclusion.

title: Vendor SLA Breach During Peak Patient Intake Hours
description: Cloud inference endpoint SLA is 99.5%. During Monday 8-10AM patient intake surge, historical patterns show 3-4x normal load — SLA may not hold.
mitigation: Auto-scaling group configured with min 3 replicas. Load test at 2x peak scheduled Wk 5. Alerting dashboard live with PagerDuty integration.

title: Epic Sandbox Remains Stable Through UAT
description: We assume Epic's UAT sandbox environment will remain available and representative of production behavior throughout Wk 4-5 integration testing.
mitigation: Monitor Epic support tickets. Mock server built as offline fallback for unit tests. Sandbox outage contingency: extend UAT window by 3 days.

title: Clinical Team 20% Time Allocation for Validation
description: Dr. Joffe's team will maintain 20% time availability for model validation workshops and sign-off activities in Wk 5-6.
mitigation: Calendar blocks confirmed by CMO. Validation activities condensed into structured 4-hour sessions rather than ad-hoc reviews. Backup reviewer identified.

title: HIPAA BAA Executed Before Patient Data Enters Pilot
description: All Business Associate Agreements with cloud infrastructure and SaaS vendors must be signed before any live patient data is processed.
mitigation: BAA executed with Epic, Azure, and Twilio on Wk 2. Verified by legal team. No additional vendors planned.

title: IT Firewall Allows Outbound API Calls to Cloud Endpoints
description: We assume hospital IT will permit outbound HTTPS calls to Azure inference endpoints without requiring VPN tunneling, which would add 80-120ms latency per call.
mitigation: IT architecture review meeting scheduled Wk 4. VPN-compatible inference path built as contingency. Latency budget analysis completed — VPN path still within acceptable SLA.

title: Epic Production Credentials Delivery Delayed 2 Weeks
description: Hospital IT procurement process requires a separate vendor approval workflow for Epic production credentials. This was not scoped in the project plan, causing a 2-week slip on EHR integration milestone.
mitigation: CTO escalation submitted Wk 3. IT procurement fast-track process initiated. Credentials expected Wk 5. UAT proceeding on sandbox in parallel to minimize schedule impact.

title: Patient Journey State Map 30% Incomplete
description: Clinical operations team has not finalized 11 of 38 patient touchpoints needed to trigger automation rules. Automation workstream cannot proceed to build until map is complete.
mitigation: Facilitated workshop scheduled with clinical ops for Wk 4. Identified clinical ops liaison to own the remaining touchpoint definitions. Automation build can proceed in parallel on 27 confirmed triggers.

title: 28% of Historical Appointment Data Missing (2019-2021)
description: Data warehouse migration from legacy system omitted appointment records from 3 fiscal years. Model trained on incomplete history may underperform for seasonal patterns.
mitigation: Backfill process initiated from archived system; 60% recovered as of Wk 4. Model retrain scheduled once full dataset available. Interim model validated acceptable (AUC 0.87) on available data.

title: Development VM Provisioning Delayed by IT Approval Queue
description: Developer VMs took 10 business days to provision due to IT security review backlog. Engineers worked on local environments for Wk 1-2.
mitigation: VMs provisioned Wk 2. No net impact on delivery timeline. Pre-approved VM templates created for future provisioning requests.

title: Epic API Sandbox & Production Access
description: Full read/write Epic FHIR API access is required for EHR integration UAT. Sandbox granted Wk 2; production access dependent on IT procurement.
mitigation: Sandbox access in use for UAT. Production access escalation in progress. Contingency: extend integration UAT window by 1 week if credentials arrive Wk 5.

title: HIPAA Business Associate Agreement Execution
description: Signed BAA required with all vendors processing PHI before live patient data can enter any environment.
mitigation: Completed Wk 2. Documented and filed with compliance team.

title: Hospital InfoSec Cloud Architecture Review
description: Information Security team must formally approve cloud infrastructure design (Azure VNet, IAM roles, encryption at rest/transit) before production provisioning can begin.
mitigation: Review in progress. Preliminary feedback positive. Expected approval by end of Wk 4. All infrastructure held in staging until approval granted.

title: Executive Sponsor Go-Live Date Confirmation
description: CTO must formally confirm the Wk 8 go-live date before launch communications are drafted and distributed to staff and patients.
mitigation: Steering committee meeting scheduled for Wk 4. Go/no-go readiness report will be presented. Date confirmation expected then.
